commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: No automated repair is indicated; the branch needs contributor-supplied live Kimi proof and maintainer validation rather than code changes.; Cleared: The diff only changes Kimi provider stream normalization and a colocated test; I found no concrete security or supply-chain regression. (sha=14eeb5713917bed2e7b49c8697174c527af09208)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Kimi stream normalization to rewrite `stopReason: "stop"` to `toolUse` only for assistant messages with parsed tagged tool-call text, and adds a non-assistant regression test.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current `main` rewrites `stopReason` after any parsed Kimi tagged tool-call content without checking `role`, and the new non-assistant fixture would catch that path. I did not run it because this review had to keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is indicated; the branch needs contributor-supplied live Kimi proof and maintainer validation rather than code changes.

target.title: Fix Kimi tool-call rewriting stop reason handling
target.body:
Proof: focused Kimi regression run passed (extensions/kimi-coding/stream.test.ts): 1 file, 17 tests.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Prevented Kimi response normalization from rewriting assistant-origin tool-call messages so that stopReason stays on the assistant path instead of being treated as a generic tool-initiated completion.
Real environment tested: Real OpenClaw checkout on a local Windows environment with actual project build/test tooling (`pnpm`, vitest config for extension providers) after patching `extensions/kimi-coding/stream.ts`.
Exact steps or command run after the patch:
- Prepare include file with `kimi-coding/stream.test.ts`.
- Set `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE` to that include file.
- Run `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest.extension-providers.config.ts`.
After-fix evidence: Terminal output from the real run included `PASS extensions/kimi-coding/stream.test.ts (17 tests)` and the expected regression assertions for assistant tool-call handling.
Observed result after fix: Assistant-role messages that contain tool calls remain on the intended assistant flow with `stopReason` preserved as expected; user-facing behavior no longer shows the assistant message rewritten to `toolUsed` stop reason in this regression scope.
What was not tested: No screenshot/video terminal capture was collected, and full cross-platform/real-provider end-to-end replay was not run in this proof pass.
target.author: neronarada
